desc: HTTP requests have no timeout, so the program can hang indefinitely when the service or network stalls.
Responses are parsed without raise_for_status(), allowing HTTP error payloads to be treated as valid application data.
Fetch failures return empty lists, making outages indistinguishable from valid empty responses and potentially producing the misleading result "No results found".
process_data assumes decoded responses are lists of dictionaries with string fields, so unexpected or malformed JSON can cause uncaught AttributeError, TypeError, or KeyError exceptions.
GLOBAL_RESULTS is never cleared, so repeated calls to process_data() or main() in the same process accumulate duplicate and stale results.
Catching Exception in each fetch function masks programming and data-decoding defects as ordinary retrieval failures, hindering diagnosis and allowing execution with invalid state.
